text:
Once upon a time in a quiet village lived a boy named Oliver. He longed to become an adventurer, chasing legends around the world. Every weekend, Oliver would strap on his old wooden shoes, load his sturdy backpack, and set out into the unknown.

One day, while practicing his map-reading skills, Oliver stumbled upon a small, glowing orb rolling along a dirt path. Intrigued, he caught it and discovered it was a map! It showed a forgotten castle deep in the woods, said to hide treasures beyond imagination. Excited but cautious, Oliver decided to explore.

With each careful step, he navigated through thickets and over rivers. When he finally reached the castle gates, the air was thick with mystery. A giant owl perched atop a weathered tower shadowed him as he peeked through the gaps.

Inside, the castle was darker than twilight. Cobwebs draped every conceivable corner. Oliver searched every nook and cranny, each finding something astonishing—a golden
